title: Novel Robust Photoplethysmogram-Based Authentication

data.dataset_name: Combined dataset (IEEE TBME Respiratory Rate Benchmark, Real-World PPG, BioSec1. Lab PPG, and local dataset)

method.input_representation: multiwavelet-based feature extraction mapping to a latent space

evaluation.context: Achieved at the authentication stage using the proposed GHM multiwavelet and autoencoder system

evaluation_protocol: Ten-fold cross-validation; 12 authorized users and 108 unauthorized users per fold

reported_limitations: difficulty to acquire real-world biometric data
short test signal sequences may not contain a complete template

usability.usability_issues: Potential impracticality if required sample size for registration/training is too large

reproducibility.benchmark_used: IEEE TBME Respiratory Rate Benchmark dataset, Real-World PPG dataset, BioSec1. Lab PPG dataset

key_contribution: Proposed a PPG authentication system using GHM multiwavelet feature extraction and an autoencoder for dimensionality reduction
Developed a compact, smart-watch-sized wearable prototype for real-time authentication
main_strength: Higher accuracy/lower EER compared to scalar-wavelet and deep learning baselines
Low execution time making it suitable for wearables
main_weakness: Lack of active spoofing/presentation attack testing
Reliance on a small local dataset for part of the evaluation
insights.notes_for_review: The paper emphasizes the benefit of multiwavelets over scalar-wavelets in capturing richer biological information for PPG signals.
